MINI MINUTES: PLTFS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE INJUNCTION:
APPEAR: David J. Millstein, Esq. 348-0348 for pltfs & moving parties; James O. Devereaux, Esq. 782-2680 for deft & opposing party.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling (GRANT INJUNCTION IN PART. DEFENDANT TO CONDUCT VOTE WITH REGARD TO FIRE ALARM UPGRADE IN ACCORDANCE WITH CC 1366(b). UPGRADE IS NOT "EMERGENCY SITUATION" WITHIN MEANING OF CC 1366(b). VOTE ON HALLWAY ASSESSMENT PROPER UNDER CC 1366(b). CC&R PREEMPTED) and sets bond in the amount of $5,000.00 without prejudice. (302/AJR/PB) Court Reporter Leland Batara, CSR#3759
